extraction.textPreview: Client shall pay all invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt. Late payments shall incur interest at a rate of 1.5% per month, or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is lower Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice. If the Consultant fails to deliver the promised services, the Company may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice I

report.summary: This service contract is assessed as critical risk, with 3 critical clauses and 1 warning clauses. The report prioritizes disproportionate liability, ambiguous drafting, loophole-prone overrides, and negotiable remediation paths. The highest-risk issue is liability language in clause-004, scored 72/100. Counterparty credibility is rated moderate at 58/100.
report.executiveLegalSummary: Overall legal posture is critical. If disputed, the strongest litigation pressure points are: General: A court is likely to enforce indemnity only to the extent the scope is clear, causation is proven, and the loss category fits the clause. General: A court will likely ask whether notice, cure rights, and the stated trigger were actually satisfied before upholding termination. General: A court will likely ask whether notice, cure rights, and the stated trigger were actually satisfied before upholding termination.
keyVulnerabilities: clause-004: A court is likely to enforce indemnity only to the extent the scope is clear, causation is proven, and the loss category fits the clause.
clause-003: A court will likely ask whether notice, cure rights, and the stated trigger were actually satisfied before upholding termination.
clause-002: A court will likely ask whether notice, cure rights, and the stated trigger were actually satisfied before upholding termination.
clause-005: A court would likely enforce the commercial core of the clause while trimming ambiguous or overreaching edges.
recommendations: No material risk remediation identified beyond standard legal review.
Reduce late-payment interest or add a grace period before default charges apply.
Require notice, cure rights, and a defined termination trigger.
Cap liability and exclude indirect or unforeseeable damages.
Limit indemnity to third-party claims caused by the supplier's proven fault.
Clarify ownership carve-outs for pre-existing IP, tools, and reusable know-how.
Validate any critical clause with jurisdiction-specific counsel before signature.
Compare risk concentration against previous agreements in the contract memory store.

elements.text: Client shall pay all invoices within thirty (30) days of receipt.

Late payments shall incur interest at a rate of 1.5% per month, or the maximum rate permitted by law, whichever is lower Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days' written notice.

If the Consultant fails to deliver the promised services, the Company may terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice In no event shall Provider's total liability exceed the fees paid by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the claim.

Provider is not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages (such as lost profits) Supplier shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Buyer against all lawsuits, claims, or costs (including legal fees) arising from defective products provided by Supplier.
Upon full payment, all work product, including creative concepts, designs, and code, shall be deemed 'work made for hire' and owned exclusively by the Client.

Upon full payment, all work product, including creative concepts, designs, and code, shall be deemed 'work made for hire' and owned exclusively by the Client.
